Moose Lake State Hospital, fourth hospital insane Minnesota, built public works administration project 1936-1938.
Massive brick buildings created rather somber interpretation Colonial Revival style which certainly affected Depression.
hospital designed independent community, with administration, medical center, receiving wards women, kitchen, bakery, laundry, powerhouse sewage disposal units, four dormitories patients, nurses' home, superintendent's residence.
Early treatments used hospital included insulin electroshock, hydrotherapy, physiotherapy.
1950s lobotomies were used some patients.
1959, when Sandstone State Hospital closed, alcoholic program transferred Moose Lake.
1961, treatment alcoholism specialization Moose Lake.
1966 program adolescents begun, which some participants attended public school gained high school credits.
Also 1966 hospital's medical/surgical wards were closed.
1972 hospital began long term chemical dependency program 1984 implemented collaborative chemical dependency program conjunction with Northland Mental Health Center Grand Rapids.
With closing Rochester State Hospital 1982, Moose Lake received fifty patients, began development specialized geriatric (psycho-geriatric) program.
1935 Minnesota legislature established commission five members appointed governor locate fourth state hospital northeastern section state (Laws 1935 c383).
commission selected land adjacent village Moose Lake Carlton County.
legislature, special session 1935-1936, confirmed location directed State Board Control purchase land proceed with construction building (Laws
Sess.
1935, sl-12).
Construction began November 1936, hospital accepted first patients, transfers from Fergus Falls Peter state hospitals, 1938.
formal opening held June
first patients directly admitted from probate courts arrived hospital August 1938.
1949 buildings male female geriatric patients combination auditorium, library, occupational therapy building were constructed.

Mary's University School Jan. dedicated $1.6 million high-tech courtroom, designed give students opportunity learn advocacy most advanced university courtrooms country.
courtroom made possible through generous support many donors, including $300,000 challenge grant from AT&T Foundation, well firms, alumni, Alumni Association, Mary's University, faculty staff school.
original courtroom constructed 1960s when school moved from downtown Antonio main university campus, received only minor cosmetic enhancements over past years.
components courtroom include latest computer audiovisual technologies with computer monitors each counsel table judges' bench, sophisticated sound video systems, document cameras plasma screens viewing exhibits offered into evidence.
These features allow hands-on learning experiences well ability videotape students evaluation feedback.
Additionally, technology provides access live webcasting remote transmissions trials hearings from federal state courts well video demand archival state federal court proceedings.
courtroom opens theater-style configuration which courtroom serves "center stage" when partitions open.
Those partitions closed make three classrooms opened accommodate large area with seating approximately people.

became part Mary's University 1934.
